algorithms.title: Radiological/Nuclear Casualty Management
algorithms.description: Responder protection and casualty care at a radiological or nuclear event.
steps: Protect responders with time, distance, and shielding; wear standard PPE and use radiation monitoring.
Remember PPE prevents contamination but does not stop penetrating gamma radiation — distance and shielding do.
Assess for life-threatening injury: treat airway, breathing, and hemorrhage FIRST, before decontamination.
Decontaminate once stable: remove clothing (most contamination), then wash gently with soap and water from wounds outward.
Address internal contamination via wounds/orifices; consider potassium iodide ONLY for radioiodine exposure.
Estimate severity with time-to-vomiting and lymphocyte-count trends; stage acute radiation syndrome.
Reassure and educate: irradiated (uncontaminated) patients are not a radiation hazard to others; manage worried-well and psychological casualties.

algorithms.title: Distinguishing Exposure From Contamination
algorithms.description: The decision that drives responder risk and patient management.
steps: Ask: was the patient only in a radiation field (irradiation) or is radioactive material present on/in them (contamination)?
Irradiation only, contamination survey negative → patient is NOT radioactive; provide normal care.
External contamination → material on skin/clothing; remove clothing and wash; responders use standard precautions.
Internal contamination → material inhaled/ingested/embedded; manage medically and involve specialists.
In all cases, treat life-threatening injuries before decontamination.
